agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "Parcel delivery logistics platform handling shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. Core workflow: create shipment with addresses, transition through states, charge based on weight bracket.",
    "bounded_context": "Shipment Management — covers shipment creation, state transitions, weight validation, and weight-bracket classification for pricing.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Shipment", "definition": "A parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states." },
      { "term": "Pickup Address", "definition": "The origin location where the parcel is collected from the sender." },
      { "term": "Delivery Address", "definition": "The destination location where the parcel is handed to the recipient." },
      { "term": "Shipment State", "definition": "The current lifecycle phase of a shipment: Pending, In Transit, or Delivered." },
      { "term": "Pending", "definition": "Initial state after shipment creation; the parcel has not yet been picked up for transport." },
      { "term": "In Transit", "definition": "The parcel has been picked up and is actively being transported toward the delivery address." },
      { "term": "Delivered", "definition": "Terminal state indicating the parcel has been handed off at the delivery address. Irreversible." },
      { "term": "Weight", "definition": "The physical mass of the parcel in kilograms, used for validation and pricing classification." },
      { "term": "Weight Bracket", "definition": "A pricing tier determined by parcel weight: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], or Freight (>50 kg)." },
      { "term": "Standard", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg." },
      { "term": "Heavy", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg." },
      { "term": "Freight", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A shipment without both endpoints is physically meaningless — there is no origin or destination. This is a data integrity constraint; the entity is corrupt without it.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to create a shipment with a null or empty pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Shipment state transitions follow a strict forward-only lifecycle: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. No backward transitions are permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "State regression (e.g., In Transit → Pending) violates physical reality of the delivery process and would corrupt audit trails, tracking data, and downstream billing events.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment in state In Transit cannot transition to Pending. A shipment in state Delivered cannot transition to any other state."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Delivered is a terminal state — no further state changes are allowed.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Delivery is a real-world event that has occurred. Reversing it would break billing records, proof-of-delivery, and chain-of-custody integrity. The system state must reflect physical reality.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment must be rejected, regardless of the target state."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must be strictly greater than 0 kg and must not exceed 1000 kg.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Zero or negative weight is physically impossible for a real parcel. The 1000 kg upper bound is a physical/operational constraint — equipment, vehicles, and safety limits enforce this ceiling. Violating it means the system accepted a shipment it cannot physically fulfill.",
      "testable_assertion": "Shipment creation with weight <= 0 or weight > 1000 must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Weight bracket classification: Standard for weight in (0, 10] kg, Heavy for weight in (10, 50] kg, Freight for weight in (50, 1000] kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The bracket thresholds (10 kg, 50 kg) and tier names are business pricing policy — a VP could adjust these thresholds without corrupting the system. The concept of bracket-based pricing is stable, but the specific boundaries are policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "A 10 kg shipment is classified as Standard. A 10.01 kg shipment is classified as Heavy. A 50 kg shipment is classified as Heavy. A 50.01 kg shipment is classified as Freight."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "A shipment in Pending state can only transition to In Transit.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Skipping states (e.g., Pending → Delivered) would bypass physical transport steps and break tracking, audit, and operational workflows. State integrity requires sequential progression.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment in Pending state that attempts to transition directly to Delivered must be rejected."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A valid pickup address and delivery address and a weight of 5 kg",
      "when": "A shipment is created",
      "then": "The shipment is created in Pending state, classified as Standard weight bracket"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A missing or empty pickup address",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating pickup address is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A missing or empty delivery address",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating delivery address is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A shipment in Pending state",
      "when": "The shipment is transitioned to In Transit",
      "then": "The state changes to In Transit successfully"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "An attempt is made to transition it back to Pending",
      "then": "The transition is rejected with an error indicating backward state transitions are not allowed"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "The shipment is transitioned to Delivered",
      "then": "The state changes to Delivered successfully"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A shipment in Delivered state",
      "when": "Any state transition is attempted (to Pending or In Transit)",
      "then": "The transition is rejected with an error indicating Delivered is a terminal state"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A shipment creation request with weight of 0 kg",
      "when": "The shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A shipment creation request with weight of -5 kg",
      "when": "The shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A shipment creation request with weight of 1001 kg",
      "when": "The shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating weight cannot exceed 1000 kg"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of exactly 10 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of 10.01 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Heavy"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of exactly 50 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Heavy"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-14",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of 50.01 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Freight"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-15",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of exactly 1000 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is determined",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Freight and creation succeeds"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-16",
      "given": "A shipment in Pending state",
      "when": "An attempt is made to transition directly to Delivered",
      "then": "The transition is rejected — shipments must pass through In Transit first"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Weight is exactly at bracket boundary (10 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Standard — the boundary is inclusive on the lower bracket (0, 10]." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is exactly at bracket boundary (50 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Heavy — the boundary is inclusive on the middle bracket (10, 50]." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is exactly 1000 kg", "expected_behavior": "Accepted and classified as Freight — 1000 kg is the inclusive upper limit." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is a very small positive number (e.g., 0.001 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Accepted and classified as Standard — weight is positive and within range. However, operationally suspicious; consider a minimum practical weight warning." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup address and delivery address are identical", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not prohibit this. Accept creation, but flag as a potential data entry error — same-address delivery is physically nonsensical for most courier operations." },
    { "scenario": "Attempting to re-deliver an already Delivered shipment", "expected_behavior": "Rejected — Delivered is terminal. A new shipment must be created for return or re-delivery." },
    { "scenario": "Concurrent state transitions on the same shipment", "expected_behavior": "Only one transition should succeed; the system must enforce state consistency under concurrent access." },
    { "scenario": "Weight provided with excessive decimal precision (e.g., 10.000000001 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Bracket classification must handle floating-point precision correctly — define a rounding policy or use fixed-precision decimals." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Weight bracket misclassification directly impacts revenue — underclassifying a Heavy parcel as Standard means undercharging. Correct bracket enforcement is critical to pricing accuracy.",
    "operational": "Strict state machine prevents parcels from regressing in the pipeline, ensuring dispatch, tracking, and proof-of-delivery workflows remain coherent. Prevents phantom re-dispatches of already-delivered parcels.",
    "user": "Senders get predictable pricing via clear weight brackets. Recipients and senders both benefit from reliable shipment tracking that never shows impossible state regressions.",
    "risk": "Primary risk is weight boundary precision errors leading to systematic pricing errors at scale. Secondary risk is state machine bypass in edge cases (concurrent updates, API misuse) leading to operational confusion.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Senders (Shippers)", "impact": "Must provide valid addresses and accurate weight. Pricing is determined by weight bracket at creation." },
      { "group": "Recipients", "impact": "Tracking accuracy depends on correct state transitions. Delivered status is authoritative and final." },
      { "group": "Dispatch/Operations Team", "impact": "Relies on state machine integrity to manage pickup queues and delivery routes. Backward transitions would corrupt scheduling." },
      { "group": "Finance/Billing", "impact": "Revenue depends on correct weight bracket classification. Terminal Delivered state triggers final invoicing." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Must handle cases where customers want to modify Delivered shipments — policy must be clear that a new shipment is required." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 78,
    "reasoning": "The spec is clear on core state machine rules and weight constraints. Bracket boundaries are specified but the boundary inclusivity (is 10 kg Standard or Heavy?) is ambiguous — I have made a reasonable domain assumption (inclusive on the lower bracket) but the PM should confirm. No mention of cancellation states, partial delivery, or return flows, which are common in real logistics but out of scope for this spec.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "State machine directionality: Pending → In Transit → Delivered, no backward transitions",
      "Delivered is terminal and irreversible",
      "Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg",
      "Three weight brackets exist with boundaries at 10 kg and 50 kg",
      "Both pickup and delivery addresses are required for creation"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Exact bracket boundary inclusivity — is exactly 10 kg Standard or Heavy? Spec says '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy' which is ambiguous at the boundary",
      "Whether Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit) is allowed — spec only explicitly forbids In Transit → Pending, but sequential progression is implied",
      "Whether weight can be changed after shipment creation",
      "Cancellation flow — no cancelled state is mentioned",
      "Address validation rules beyond presence — format, geocoding, serviceability"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Bracket boundaries are inclusive on the upper end of each lower tier: Standard = (0, 10], Heavy = (10, 50], Freight = (50, 1000]",
      "State transitions must be sequential — Pending cannot skip directly to Delivered",
      "Weight is immutable after shipment creation (not stated but consistent with physical reality and pricing integrity)",
      "Initial state of a newly created shipment is Pending",
      "There is no Cancelled state in the current model"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Weight bracket boundary inclusivity is ambiguous. The spec says '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy' — a parcel weighing exactly 10 kg could be either Standard or Heavy. Same ambiguity at the 50 kg boundary.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance/Billing", "Senders (Shippers)"],
      "options": ["A: Boundaries are inclusive on the lower tier (10 kg = Standard, 50 kg = Heavy)", "B: Boundaries are inclusive on the upper tier (10 kg = Heavy, 50 kg = Freight)", "C: Use exclusive upper bounds with a defined precision (e.g., Standard is weight < 10.00 kg)"],
      "recommendation": "Option A — inclusive on the lower tier — is most customer-friendly and industry-standard. But PM must confirm to avoid systematic pricing errors."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No cancellation or return flow is defined. In real logistics, shipments in Pending or In Transit state are frequently cancelled or returned. The current model has no way to handle this without violating the forward-only state machine.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Senders (Shippers)", "Customer Support", "Dispatch/Operations Team"],
      "options": ["A: Add a Cancelled state reachable from Pending and In Transit", "B: Cancellation is out of scope for v1 — handle via separate process", "C: Add both Cancelled and Returned states"],
      "recommendation": "Clarify with PM whether cancellation is deferred to a future iteration or needs to be modeled now. For v1, Option B is safe if cancellation volume is low."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
